sEn: Kimi-Audio is an open-source audio foundation model achieving state-of-the-art performance in understanding, generation, and conversation.
sZh: Kimi-Audio 是一个开源音频基础模型，在理解、生成和对话方面达到最先进水平。
absEn: We present Kimi-Audio, an open-source audio foundation model that excels in audio understanding, generation, and conversation. We detail the practices in building Kimi-Audio, including model architecture, data curation, training recipe, inference deployment, and evaluation. Specifically, we leverage a 12.5Hz audio tokenizer, design a novel LLM-based architecture with continuous features as input and discrete tokens as output, and develop a chunk-wise streaming detokenizer based on flow matching. We curate a pre-training dataset that consists of more than 13 million hours of audio data covering a wide range of modalities including speech, sound, and music, and build a pipeline to construct high-quality and diverse post-training data. Initialized from a pre-trained LLM, Kimi-Audio is continual pre-trained on both audio and text data with several carefully designed tasks, and then fine-tuned to support a diverse of audio-related tasks. Extensive evaluation shows that Kimi-Audio achieves state-of-the-art performance on a range of audio benchmarks including speech recognition, audio understanding, audio question answering, and speech conversation. We release the codes, model checkpoints, as well as the evaluation toolkits in https://github.com/MoonshotAI/Kimi-Audio.
absZh: 我们介绍了 Kimi-Audio，一个在音频理解、生成和对话方面表现出色的开源音频基础模型。我们详细阐述了构建 Kimi-Audio 的实践，包括模型架构、数据整理、训练方案、推理部署和评估。具体来说，我们利用了一个 12.5Hz 的音频分词器，设计了一种新颖的基于 LLM 的架构，以连续特征作为输入、离散标记作为输出，并开发了一种基于流匹配的块式流式解分词器。我们整理了一个包含超过 1300 万小时音频数据的预训练数据集，涵盖语音、声音和音乐等多种模态，并构建了一个用于构建高质量、多样化后训练数据的流程。Kimi-Audio 从预训练的 LLM 初始化，通过几个精心设计的任务在音频和文本数据上持续预训练，然后进行微调以支持多种音频相关任务。大量评估表明，Kimi-Audio 在多项音频基准测试中取得了最先进的性能，包括语音识别、音频理解、音频问答和语音对话。我们在 https://github.com/MoonshotAI/Kimi-Audio 上发布了代码、模型检查点和评估工具包。

contrib.en: Proposes a novel LLM-based architecture with continuous features as input and discrete tokens as output for audio.
contrib.zh: 提出了一种新颖的基于LLM的架构，以连续特征作为输入、离散标记作为输出，用于音频处理。

contrib.en: Curates a pre-training dataset of over 13 million hours of audio covering speech, sound, and music.
contrib.zh: 整理了超过1300万小时的音频预训练数据集，涵盖语音、声音和音乐。

contrib.en: Develops a chunk-wise streaming detokenizer based on flow matching for low-latency speech generation.
contrib.zh: 开发了基于流匹配的逐块流式解标记器，用于低延迟语音生成。

contrib.en: Achieves state-of-the-art performance on speech recognition, audio understanding, audio question answering, and speech conversation benchmarks.
contrib.zh: 在语音识别、音频理解、音频问答和语音对话基准测试中取得了最先进的性能。

contrib.en: Releases open-source codes, model checkpoints, and evaluation toolkits to the community.
contrib.zh: 向社区开源了代码、模型检查点和评估工具包。

limits.en: Pre-training relies on ASR transcription, neglecting paralinguistic and environmental audio information.
limits.zh: 预训练依赖ASR转录，忽略了副语言和环境音频信息。

limits.en: Audio representations (semantic tokens) focus on transcription, missing rich acoustic details.
limits.zh: 音频表示（语义标记）侧重于转录，缺少丰富的声学细节。

limits.en: Heavy dependence on ASR and TTS for training data, limiting performance ceiling.
limits.zh: 严重依赖ASR和TTS生成训练数据，限制了性能上限。

limits.en: Model evaluated mainly on English and Mandarin; generalization to other languages is limited.
limits.zh: 模型主要在英语和普通话上评估，对其他语言的泛化能力有限。

items.en: We present Kimi-Audio, an open-source audio foundation model that excels in audio understanding, generation, and conversation. We detail the practices in building Kimi-Audio, including model architecture, data curation, training recipe, inference deployment, and evaluation. Specifically, we leverage a 12.512.5Hz audio tokenizer, design a novel LLM-based architecture with continuous features as input and discrete tokens as output, and develop a chunk-wise streaming detokenizer based on flow matching. We curate a pre-training dataset that consists of more than 1313 million hours of audio data covering a wide range of modalities including speech, sound, and music, and build a pipeline to construct high-quality and diverse post-training data. Initialized from a pre-trained LLM, Kimi-Audio is continual pre-trained on both audio and text data with several carefully designed tasks, and then fine-tuned to support a diverse of audio-related tasks. Extensive evaluation shows that Kimi-Audio achieves state-of-the-art performance on a range of audio benchmarks including speech recognition, audio understanding, audio question answering, and speech conversation. We release the codes, model checkpoints, as well as the evaluation toolkits in https://github.com/MoonshotAI/Kimi-Audio.
items.zh: 我们提出Kimi-Audio，一个开源的音频基础模型，在音频理解、生成和对话方面表现出色。我们详细介绍了构建Kimi-Audio的实践，包括模型架构、数据整理、训练方案、推理部署和评估。具体来说，我们利用一个12.5Hz的音频分词器，设计了一种新颖的基于LLM的架构，以连续特征作为输入、离散token作为输出，并开发了基于流匹配的逐块流式解分词器。我们整理了一个预训练数据集，包含超过1300万小时的音频数据，涵盖语音、声音和音乐等多种模态，并构建了一个流程来生成高质量且多样化的后训练数据。Kimi-Audio从预训练的LLM初始化，通过几个精心设计的任务在音频和文本数据上持续预训练，然后进行微调以支持多种音频相关任务。广泛的评估表明，Kimi-Audio在包括语音识别、音频理解、音频问答和语音对话在内的一系列音频基准测试中取得了最先进的性能。我们在https://github.com/MoonshotAI/Kimi-Audio上发布了代码、模型检查点和评估工具包。

items.capEn: Figure 1: Performance of Kimi-Audio and previous audio langauge models including Qwen2-Audio [11], Baichuan-Audio [41], Step-Audio [28], and Qwen2.5-Omni [73] on various benchmarks.
items.capZh: 图1：Kimi-Audio与之前的音频语言模型（包括Qwen2-Audio [11]、Baichuan-Audio [41]、Step-Audio [28]和Qwen2.5-Omni [73]）在各种基准测试上的性能。

items.en: Audio plays an indispensable role in human daily life, such as environment perception, speech conversation, emotion expression, and music appreciation, and is an important topic in artificial general intelligence. Traditional audio modeling, constrained by the development of artificial intelligence, handles each audio processing task (e.g., speech recognition, emotion recognition, sound event detection, and speech conversation) separately. However, audio is naturally sequential and speech has strict correspondence with text, which makes it suitable to take advantage of the rapid progress in large language models (LLMs) in audio modeling. Just as natural language processing has experienced, audio processing evolves quickly from separate models for separate tasks to a universal model handling a variety of tasks.
items.zh: 音频在人类日常生活中扮演着不可或缺的角色，例如环境感知、语音对话、情感表达和音乐欣赏，并且是通用人工智能中的一个重要课题。受人工智能发展的限制，传统的音频建模分别处理每个音频处理任务（例如语音识别、情感识别、声音事件检测和语音对话）。然而，音频本质上是序列化的，并且语音与文本有严格的对应关系，这使得它适合利用大语言模型（LLM）在音频建模中的快速进展。正如自然语言处理所经历的那样，音频处理从针对不同任务的独立模型迅速发展为处理多种任务的通用模型。

items.en: For example, pioneer works introduce language models in audio generation [3, 77], audio understanding [10, 63, 9], speech recognition [58, 87], speech synthesis [70, 80], and end-to-end speech conversation [29, 14]. However, previous works fall short of building a universal audio foundation model for a variety of audio processing tasks in several aspects: 1) not universal but only focus on a specific type of tasks, such as audio understanding [10, 11, 21, 63, 88, 35], audio generation [45, 77], or speech conversation [14, 84]; 2) not much emphasis on audio pre-training but only fine-tuning an LLM on downstream audio tasks [10, 63, 9]; 3) no access to source codes and checkpoints, with limited value to the community [29, 7].
items.zh: 例如，先驱工作将语言模型引入音频生成 [3, 77]、音频理解 [10, 63, 9]、语音识别 [58, 87]、语音合成 [70, 80] 和端到端语音对话 [29, 14]。然而，先前的工作在构建用于多种音频处理任务的通用音频基础模型方面存在不足，体现在以下几个方面：1）不通用，只关注特定类型的任务，如音频理解 [10, 11, 21, 63, 88, 35]、音频生成 [45, 77] 或语音对话 [14, 84]；2）不重视音频预训练，仅在下游音频任务上微调LLM [10, 63, 9]；3）无法获取源代码和检查点，对社区的价值有限 [29, 7]。

items.en: In this report, we present Kimi-Audio, an open-source audio foundation model that handles a variety of audio processing tasks. We detail our effort in building a state-of-the-art (SOTA) audio foundation model in three espects: architecture, data, and training.
items.zh: 在本报告中，我们提出Kimi-Audio，一个开源的音频基础模型，能够处理多种音频处理任务。我们详细介绍了在构建最先进（SOTA）音频基础模型方面所做的努力，涉及三个方面：架构、数据和训练。

items.en: Architecture. Our model consists of three components: an audio tokenizer and detokenizer as audio I/O, and an audio LLM as the core processing part (see Section 2.1). We use discrete semantic audio tokens as the basic representation for both the input and output of the audio LLM. Meanwhile, we concatenate the semantic audio token with continuous acoustic vectors in the input to enhance perception capability, and concatenate with discrete text tokens in the output to enhance the generation capability. In this way, we can achieve good audio perception and generation capabilities at the same time, facilitate universal audio modeling. We reduce the number of token per second in audio to bridge the gap between text and audio sequence and set the compression rate of both the semantic and acoustic audio tokens as 12.512.5Hz. The detailed design of the audio tokenizer for both discrete semantic tokens and continuous acoustic vectors, as well as the generation of both discrete semantic tokens and text tokens are introduced in Section 2.2 and 2.3 respectively.
items.zh: 架构。我们的模型由三个组件组成：一个音频分词器和解分词器作为音频输入/输出，以及一个音频LLM作为核心处理部分（见第2.1节）。我们使用离散的语义音频token作为音频LLM输入和输出的基本表示。同时，我们在输入中将语义音频token与连续声学向量拼接以增强感知能力，并在输出中与离散文本token拼接以增强生成能力。通过这种方式，我们可以同时实现良好的音频感知和生成能力，促进通用音频建模。我们降低了音频每秒的token数量，以弥合文本和音频序列之间的差距，并将语义和声学音频token的压缩率都设置为12.5Hz。第2.2节和第2.3节分别介绍了离散语义token和连续声学向量的音频分词器详细设计，以及离散语义token和文本token的生成。

items.en: Data. To achieve SOTA universal audio modeling, we need to pre-train the model on a large amount of audio data to see diverse scenarios. To this end, we crawl and process a large-scale audio pre-training dataset. We develop a data processing pipeline consisting of speech enhancement, diarization, transcription, filtering, etc, to enable high data quality (see Section 3.1). To support diverse audio processing tasks, we curate a large amount of task-specific data for supervised fine-tuning (SFT). We demonstrate an economic way to construct most of SFT data with pure open and accessible data sources and processing tools to achieve SOTA performance, without relying on any data purchase (see Section 3.2).
items.zh: 数据。为了实现最先进的通用音频建模，我们需要在大量音频数据上预训练模型，以覆盖多样化的场景。为此，我们爬取并处理了一个大规模的音频预训练数据集。我们开发了一个数据处理流程，包括语音增强、说话人分离、转录、过滤等，以确保高数据质量（见第3.1节）。为了支持多样化的音频处理任务，我们整理了大量任务特定数据用于监督微调（SFT）。我们展示了一种经济的方法，仅使用纯开放和可访问的数据源及处理工具来构建大部分SFT数据，以实现最先进的性能，而无需依赖任何数据购买（见第3.2节）。

items.en: Training. To achieve good audio understanding/generation capability while maintaining high knowledge capacity and intelligence, we initialize the audio LLM with a pre-trained LLM, and carefully design a series of pre-training tasks to fully learn the audio data and bridge the gap between text and audio. Specifically, the pre-training tasks can be divided into three categories: 1) text-only and audio-only pre-training, which aims to learn the knowledge from text and audio domains separately; 2) audio-to-text mapping, which encourages the conversion between audio and text; 3) audio-text interleaving, which further bridge the gap between text and audio (see Section 4.1). In the supervised fine-tuning stage, we develop a training recipe to improve fine-tuning efficiency and task generalization (see Section 4.2).
items.zh: 训练。为了实现良好的音频理解/生成能力，同时保持高知识容量和智能，我们使用预训练的LLM初始化音频LLM，并精心设计了一系列预训练任务，以充分学习音频数据并弥合文本和音频之间的差距。具体来说，预训练任务可以分为三类：1）纯文本和纯音频预训练，旨在分别从文本和音频领域学习知识；2）音频到文本映射，促进音频和文本之间的转换；3）音频文本交错，进一步弥合文本和音频之间的差距（见第4.1节）。在监督微调阶段，我们开发了一种训练方案，以提高微调效率和任务泛化能力（见第4.2节）。

items.en: Furthermore, we introduce the practices for deploying and serving our audio foundation model for inference in Kimi APP, as described in Section 5. Evaluating and benchmarking an audio foundation model in various downstream tasks such as speech recognition, audio understanding, and speech conversation is challenging. We encounter tricky issues in fairly comparing different audio models, such as non-standardized metric, evaluation protocol, and inference hyper-parameters. Therefore, we develop an evaluation toolkit that can faithfully evaluate audio LLMs on comprehensive benchmarks (see Section 6.1). We open-source this toolkit to facilitate a fair comparison in the community.
items.zh: 此外，我们介绍了在Kimi APP中部署和服务我们的音频基础模型进行推理的实践，如第5节所述。在各种下游任务（如语音识别、音频理解和语音对话）中评估和基准测试音频基础模型具有挑战性。我们在公平比较不同音频模型时遇到了棘手的问题，例如非标准化的指标、评估协议和推理超参数。因此，我们开发了一个评估工具包，可以在综合基准测试中忠实地评估音频LLM（见第6.1节）。我们开源了这个工具包，以促进社区中的公平比较。

items.en: Based on our evaluation toolkit, we conduct a comprehensive evaluation of Kimi-Audio and other audio LLMs on a variety of audio benchmarks (see Section 6.2). Evaluation results demonstrate that Kimi-Audio achieves SOTA performance in a series of audio tasks, including speech recognition, audio understanding, audio-to-text chat, and speech conversation. We open source the codes and checkpoints of Kimi-Audio, as well as the evaluation toolkit in https://github.com/MoonshotAI/Kimi-Audio, to boost the development of the community.
items.zh: 基于我们的评估工具包，我们在各种音频基准测试中对Kimi-Audio和其他音频LLM进行了全面评估（见第6.2节）。评估结果表明，Kimi-Audio在一系列音频任务中取得了最先进的性能，包括语音识别、音频理解、音频到文本聊天和语音对话。我们在https://github.com/MoonshotAI/Kimi-Audio上开源了Kimi-Audio的代码和检查点，以及评估工具包，以推动社区的发展。

items.capEn: Figure 2: Overview of the Kimi-Audio model architecture: (1) an audio tokenizer that extracts discrete semantic tokens and a Whisper encoder that generates continuous acoustic features; (2) an audio LLM that processes audio inputs and generates text and/or audio outputs; (3) an audio detokenizer converts audio tokens into waveforms.
items.capZh: 图2：Kimi-Audio模型架构概览：（1）一个音频分词器，提取离散语义token和一个Whisper编码器，生成连续声学特征；（2）一个音频LLM，处理音频输入并生成文本和/或音频输出；（3）一个音频解分词器，将音频token转换为波形。

items.en: Kimi-Audio is an audio foundation model designed to perform comprehensive audio understanding, generation, and conversation tasks within a unified architecture. As illustrated in Figure 2, our system comprises three primary components: (1) an audio tokenizer that converts input audio into discrete semantic tokens derived through vector quantization with a 12.512.5Hz frame rate. The audio tokenizer additionally extracts continuous acoustic vectors to enhance perception capability. (2) an audio LLM that generates semantic tokens together with text token to improve the generation capability, featuring shared transformer layers that process multimodal inputs before branching into specialized parallel heads for text and audio generation; and (3) an audio detokenizer that converts the discrete semantic tokens predicted by the audio LLM back into coherent audio waveforms using a flow matching approach. This integrated architecture enables Kimi-Audio to seamlessly handle diverse audio-language tasks from speech recognition and understanding to speech conversation within a single unified model framework.
items.zh: Kimi-Audio是一个音频基础模型，旨在统一架构内执行全面的音频理解、生成和对话任务。如图2所示，我们的系统包含三个主要组件：（1）一个音频分词器，将输入音频转换为通过向量量化得到的离散语义token，帧率为12.5Hz。音频分词器还提取连续声学向量以增强感知能力。（2）一个音频LLM，生成语义token以及文本token以提高生成能力，具有共享的Transformer层，处理多模态输入，然后分支到用于文本和音频生成的专用并行头；（3）一个音频解分词器，使用流匹配方法将音频LLM预测的离散语义token转换回连贯的音频波形。这种集成架构使Kimi-Audio能够在单个统一模型框架内无缝处理从语音识别和理解到语音对话的多种音频语言任务。

items.en: Our audio foundation model employs a hybrid audio tokenization strategy, integrating discrete semantic tokens and complementary continuous vectors of acoustic information to effectively represent speech signals for downstream tasks. This tokenization allows the model to leverage the efficiency and semantic focus of discrete tokens while benefiting from the rich acoustic details captured by continuous representations.
items.zh: 我们的音频基础模型采用混合音频分词策略，结合离散语义token和补充的连续声学信息向量，以有效表示语音信号用于下游任务。这种分词使模型能够利用离散token的效率和语义焦点，同时受益于连续表示捕获的丰富声学细节。

items.en: We incorporate the discrete semantic tokens proposed by GLM-4-Voice [84]. This component utilizes a supervised speech tokenizer derived from an automatic speech recognition (ASR) model. By introducing a vector quantization layer within the whisper encoder architecture [58], we can transform continuous speech representations into a sequence of discrete tokens at a low frame rate (i.e. 12.512.5Hz) using a single codebook.
items.zh: 我们采用了GLM-4-Voice [84]提出的离散语义token。该组件使用从自动语音识别（ASR）模型导出的监督语音分词器。通过在Whisper编码器架构 [58]中引入向量量化层，我们可以使用单个码本将连续语音表示转换为低帧率（即12.5Hz）的离散token序列。

items.en: Complementing the discrete semantic tokens, we incorporate a continuous feature representation derived from a pre-trained whisper model [58] to enhance the perception capability of our model. Since the whisper feature has a frame rate of 5050Hz, we additionally introduce an adaptor upon the whisper feature extractor to downsample the feature from 5050Hz to 12.512.5Hz. The downsampled features are added to the embeddings of discrete semantic tokens to serve as the input of the audio LLM.
items.zh: 作为离散语义token的补充，我们引入了从预训练Whisper模型 [58]导出的连续特征表示，以增强模型的感知能力。由于Whisper特征的帧率为50Hz，我们额外在Whisper特征提取器上引入了一个适配器，将特征从50Hz下采样到12.5Hz。下采样后的特征被添加到离散语义token的嵌入中，作为音频LLM的输入。

items.en: By combining discrete semantic tokens with continuous whisper features, our model benefits from efficient, semantically grounded representation and detailed acoustic modeling, providing a comprehensive foundation for diverse audio processing tasks.
items.zh: 通过将离散语义token与连续Whisper特征相结合，我们的模型受益于高效、语义基础的表示和详细的声学建模，为多样化的音频处理任务提供了全面的基础。

items.en: The core of our system is an audio LLM designed to process the audio representations generated by the tokenization strategy described in Section 2.2 and produce multimodal outputs, which include the discrete semantic tokens of audio and the corresponding text tokens to improve the generation capability.
items.zh: 我们系统的核心是一个音频LLM，旨在处理第2.2节所述分词策略生成的音频表示，并产生多模态输出，包括音频的离散语义token和相应的文本token，以提高生成能力。

items.en: To enable the model to generate both audio semantic tokens and the corresponding textual responses, we adapt the standard LLM architecture by structuring it into components with shared and specialized functions. A significant portion of the original transformer bottom layers, i.e., the first several layers, are utilized as shared layers. These layers process the input sequence and learn cross-modal representations, integrating information from both text and audio modalities present in the input or context. Based on these shared layers, the architecture diverges into two parallel heads containing transformer layers. The first head is a text head that is specifically responsible for autoregressively predicting text tokens, forming the textual output of the model. The second head is an audio head to predict the discrete audio semantic tokens. These predicted audio tokens are subsequently passed to an audio detokenizer module to synthesize the final output audio waveform.
items.zh: 为了使模型能够同时生成音频语义token和相应的文本响应，我们通过将标准LLM架构组织为具有共享和专用功能的组件来对其进行调整。原始Transformer底层的大部分，即前几层，被用作共享层。这些层处理输入序列并学习跨模态表示，整合输入或上下文中文本和音频模态的信息。基于这些共享层，架构分叉为两个包含Transformer层的并行头。第一个头是文本头，专门负责自回归预测文本token，形成模型的文本输出。第二个头是音频头，用于预测离散的音频语义token。这些预测的音频token随后被传递给音频解分词器模块，以合成最终的输出音频波形。

items.en: To take advantage of the strong language capabilities of the pre-trained text LLMs [76, 24, 13], the parameters of the shared transformer layers and the text head are initialized directly from the weights of the pre-trained text LLM. The audio head layers are initialized randomly. This initialization strategy ensures that the model retains robust text understanding and generation capabilities while learning to effectively process and generate audio information.
items.zh: 为了利用预训练文本LLM [76, 24, 13]的强大语言能力，共享Transformer层和文本头的参数直接从预训练文本LLM的权重初始化。音频头层随机初始化。这种初始化策略确保模型在保留强大的文本理解和生成能力的同时，学习有效处理和生成音频信息。

items.en: The audio detokenizer aims to generate high-quality and expressive speech conditioned on discrete semantic audio tokens. We employ the same detokenizer architecture as in MoonCast [32], which contains two parts: 1) a flow-matching module which converts 12.512.5Hz semantic tokens to 5050Hz mel-spectrograms; 2) a vocoder which generates waveforms from mel-spectrograms. To reduce speech generation latency, we design a chunk-wise streaming detokenizer. Intuitively, we can split the semantic tokens into chunks and decode them separately, which, however, in our preliminary experiments, faces an intermittent issue in the chunk boundaries. Thus, we propose a chunk-wise autoregressive streaming framework with a look-ahead mechanism.
items.zh: 音频解分词器旨在根据离散语义音频token生成高质量且富有表现力的语音。我们采用与MoonCast [32]相同的解分词器架构，包含两个部分：1）一个流匹配模块，将12.5Hz的语义token转换为50Hz的梅尔频谱图；2）一个声码器，从梅尔频谱图生成波形。为了减少语音生成延迟，我们设计了一个逐块流式解分词器。直观上，我们可以将语义token分成块并分别解码，然而，在我们的初步实验中，这面临块边界处的间歇性问题。因此，我们提出了一种带有前瞻机制的逐块自回归流式框架。

items.en: Chunk-wise Autoregressive Streaming Framework. We split the audio into chunks (e.g., 11 second per chunk): {c1,c2,…,ci,…,cN}\{c_{1},c_{2},...,c_{i},...,c_{N}\}, where NN is the number of chunks. Firstly, to match the sequence length between semantic tokens (12.512.5Hz) and mel-spectrograms (5050Hz), we upsample the semantic tokens by 44x rate. Secondly, we apply a chunk-wise causal mask during training and inference, i.e., for chunk cic_{i}, all previous chunks cjc_{j} with j<ij<i are prompts. We denote chunk cic_{i}’s mel-spectrograms as mim_{i} and the corresponding discrete semantic audio tokens as aida^{d}_{i}. The flow-matching model’s forward step will mix mim_{i} with Gaussian noise and the backward step will remove noise to obtain clean mim_{i} with condition aida^{d}_{i} and prompt cjc_{j}, where j<ij<i, and cjc_{j} contains both mjm_{j} and ajda^{d}_{j}. With this design, during inference, when the LLM generates a chunk, we employ the flow-matching model to detokenize it to obtain the mel-spectrograms. Finally, we apply a BigVGAN [38] vocoder to generate wavforms for each chunk.
items.zh: 分块自回归流式框架。我们将音频分割成块（例如，每块11秒）：{c1,c2,…,ci,…,cN}\{c_{1},c_{2},...,c_{i},...,c_{N}\}，其中NN是块的数量。首先，为了匹配语义标记（12.512.5Hz）和梅尔频谱图（5050Hz）之间的序列长度，我们将语义标记上采样4倍。其次，在训练和推理过程中，我们应用分块因果掩码，即对于块cic_{i}，所有之前的块cjc_{j}（j<ij<i）作为提示。我们将块cic_{i}的梅尔频谱图记为mim_{i}，对应的离散语义音频标记记为aida^{d}_{i}。流匹配模型的前向步骤将mim_{i}与高斯噪声混合，反向步骤则去除噪声以获得干净的mim_{i}，条件为aida^{d}_{i}和提示cjc_{j}（j<ij<i），其中cjc_{j}包含mjm_{j}和ajda^{d}_{j}。通过这种设计，在推理过程中，当LLM生成一个块时，我们使用流匹配模型对其进行解标记以获得梅尔频谱图。最后，我们应用BigVGAN [38]声码器为每个块生成波形。

items.en: Look-Ahead Mechanism. With a preliminary study, we find that the generated audio in the boundaries of chunks still has an intermittent issue. Although a long range of history context has been seen during the diffusion denoising process, the future context of the boundary position cannot be seen due to the nature of block-wise causal attention, which causes the degradation of quality. Thus, we propose a look-ahead mechanism. In detail, for chunk cic_{i}, we take the future nn (e.g. 44) semantic tokens from chunk ci+1c_{i+1} and concatenate them to the end of cic_{i} to form c^i\hat{c}_{i}. Then we detokenize c^i\hat{c}_{i} to generate the mel-spectrograms, but only retain the mel-spectrograms corresponding to cic_{i}. This mechanism is training-free and will only delay the generation of the first chunk by nn tokens.
items.zh: 前瞻机制。通过初步研究，我们发现块边界处的生成音频仍然存在间歇性问题。尽管在扩散去噪过程中已经看到了长范围的历史上下文，但由于分块因果注意力的性质，边界位置的未来上下文无法被看到，这导致了质量下降。因此，我们提出了一种前瞻机制。具体来说，对于块cic_{i}，我们从块ci+1c_{i+1}中取出未来的nn个（例如44个）语义标记，并将它们连接到cic_{i}的末尾，形成c^i\hat{c}_{i}。然后我们对c^i\hat{c}_{i}进行解标记以生成梅尔频谱图，但只保留对应于cic_{i}的梅尔频谱图。这种机制无需训练，只会将第一个块的生成延迟nn个标记。

items.capEn: Figure 3: Processing pipeline for the audio pre-training data.
items.capZh: 图3：音频预训练数据的处理流程。

items.en: Our pre-training corpus comprises both unimodal (text-only, audio-only) and multimodal (text-audio) data. The audio-only pre-training data covers a wide range of real-world scenarios, including audiobooks, podcasts, and interviews, and consists of approximately 1313 million hours of raw audio containing rich acoustic events, music, environmental sound, human vocalization, and multilingual information. The details of the text-only pre-training data can be found in [65].
items.zh: 我们的预训练语料库包括单模态（仅文本、仅音频）和多模态（文本-音频）数据。仅音频预训练数据涵盖了广泛的真实场景，包括有声书、播客和访谈，包含约1313百万小时的原始音频，其中包含丰富的声学事件、音乐、环境声音、人类发声和多语言信息。仅文本预训练数据的详细信息可在[65]中找到。

items.en: Most audio corpus contains only raw audio without corresponding transcriptions, language types, speaker annotations, and segmentation boundaries. In addition, the raw audio often contains undesired artifacts such as background noise, reverberation, and speaker overlap.
items.zh: 大多数音频语料库只包含原始音频，没有相应的转录、语言类型、说话人标注和分割边界。此外，原始音频通常包含不需要的伪影，如背景噪声、混响和说话人重叠。

items.en: Inspired by previous work [32, 81, 26], we develop an efficient automatic audio data processing pipeline to generate high-quality annotations, resulting in our multimodal (audio-text) data.
items.zh: 受先前工作[32, 81, 26]的启发，我们开发了一个高效的自动音频数据处理流程，以生成高质量的标注，从而得到我们的多模态（音频-文本）数据。

items.en: Compared to previous data processing pipelines that primarily focus on generating high-quality short audio segments without contextual information, our pipeline is designed to provide long-form audio annotations with consistent long-range context. The pipeline includes the following key components in a step-by-step manner, as shown in Figure 3 and described as follows.
items.zh: 与之前主要关注生成无上下文信息的高质量短音频片段的数据处理流程相比，我们的流程旨在提供具有一致长范围上下文的长时间音频标注。该流程按步骤包括以下关键组件，如图3所示，描述如下。

items.en: Speech Enhancement. To suppress undesired background noise and reverberation, we develop a speech enhancement model based on the Band-Split RNN (BSRNN) architecture [49], as shown in Figure 3(A). Following the same hyper-parameter configuration as in [82], the model is applied to perform 4848kHz speech enhancement. Empirically, we find that speech enhancement will remove the environmental sound and music, which can be harmful to audio understanding. Thus, we randomly choose original or enhanced audio with a ratio of 1:11:1 in the pre-training stage.
items.zh: 语音增强。为了抑制不需要的背景噪声和混响，我们基于Band-Split RNN（BSRNN）架构[49]开发了一个语音增强模型，如图3(A)所示。遵循与[82]相同的超参数配置，该模型用于执行4848kHz语音增强。经验上，我们发现语音增强会去除环境声音和音乐，这可能对音频理解有害。因此，在预训练阶段，我们以1:11:1的比例随机选择原始或增强音频。

items.en: Segmentation by Diarization. We employ a diarization-driven approach to segment long-form audio. We utilize the PyAnnote toolkit111https://github.com/pyannote/pyannote-audio for speaker diarization (Figure 3(B)), which segments the audio and assigns speaker labels. However, the raw output is sub-optimal, and thus we develop a post-processing pipeline to address the issues in the previous segmentation results:
items.zh: 通过说话人日志进行分割。我们采用基于说话人日志的方法来分割长时间音频。我们使用PyAnnote工具包111https://github.com/pyannote/pyannote-audio进行说话人日志（图3(B)），该工具分割音频并分配说话人标签。然而，原始输出并非最优，因此我们开发了一个后处理流程来解决之前分割结果中的问题：

items.en: Speaker Cluster Merging. We observe that PyAnnote sometimes assigns multiple speaker labels to the same actual speaker, which results in speaker fragmentation. We compute representative speaker embeddings for each initial cluster and merge pairs of clusters whose embeddings have a cosine similarity greater than 0.60.6, as shown in Figure 3(C).
items.zh: 说话人聚类合并。我们观察到PyAnnote有时会为同一个实际说话人分配多个说话人标签，导致说话人碎片化。我们为每个初始聚类计算代表性说话人嵌入，并合并嵌入余弦相似度大于0.60.6的聚类对，如图3(C)所示。

items.en: Chunk-based Reassignment. The initial diarization occasionally produced segments containing multiple speakers. To purify the segments, 1) we first divide all segments into 1.51.5-second chunks, and then 2) for each pair of adjacent chunks, if their cosine similarity is below 0.50.5, we treat them as belonging to different speakers and reassign each chunk to the speaker cluster with the highest similarity, as shown in Figure 3(D).
items.zh: 基于块的重分配。初始说话人日志偶尔会产生包含多个说话人的片段。为了净化片段，1）我们首先将所有片段划分为1.51.5秒的块，然后2）对于每对相邻块，如果它们的余弦相似度低于0.50.5，则将其视为属于不同说话人，并将每个块重新分配给相似度最高的说话人聚类，如图3(D)所示。

items.en: Segment Merging. The initial diarization could result in segments of highly variable and sometimes impractical lengths (shorter than 11s or longer than 100100s). So we iteratively merge adjacent segments labeled with the same speaker (after the reassignment step). The merging process terminates if the accumulated segment length exceeds 2727 seconds or the silence gap between two segments is greater than 22 seconds, as shown in Figure 3(E).
items.zh: 片段合并。初始说话人日志可能导致片段长度高度可变且有时不切实际（短于11秒或长于100100秒）。因此，我们迭代合并标记为同一说话人的相邻片段（在重分配步骤之后）。如果累积片段长度超过2727秒或两个片段之间的静音间隔大于22秒，则合并过程终止，如图3(E)所示。

items.en: The resulting segmentation from this refined diarization process provides more accurate and consistently sized speaker turns compared to the baseline diarization output.
items.zh: 与基线说话人日志输出相比，这种经过精炼的说话人日志过程产生的分割提供了更准确且大小一致的说话人轮次。

items.en: Speech Transcription. To obtain the language type and text transcription for each speech segment, we first apply the Whisper-large-v3 model [58]222https://huggingface.co/openai/whisper-large-v3 to detect the spoken language type. In this work, we retain only English and Mandarin segments for further transcription. For English segments, we directly use Whisper-large-v3 to generate both transcriptions and punctuation annotations. For Mandarin segments, we utilize the Paraformer-Zh [20] model from the FunASR toolkit333https://github.com/modelscope/FunASR to generate transcriptions along with character-level timestamps. Since Paraformer-Zh cannot output punctuation annotations, we add punctuation annotations with the following strategy: if the time gap between two consecutive characters is greater than 0.50.5 seconds but less than 1.01.0 second, we insert a “comma”; if the gap exceeds 1.01.0 second, we insert a “period”.
items.zh: 语音转录。为了获得每个语音片段的语言类型和文本转录，我们首先应用Whisper-large-v3模型[58]222https://huggingface.co/openai/whisper-large-v3检测口语语言类型。在这项工作中，我们只保留英语和普通话片段进行进一步转录。对于英语片段，我们直接使用Whisper-large-v3生成转录和标点标注。对于普通话片段，我们使用FunASR工具包333https://github.com/modelscope/FunASR中的Paraformer-Zh [20]模型生成转录以及字符级时间戳。由于Paraformer-Zh无法输出标点标注，我们采用以下策略添加标点标注：如果两个连续字符之间的时间间隔大于0.50.5秒但小于1.01.0秒，则插入“逗号”；如果间隔超过1.01.0秒，则插入“句号”。

items.en: Implementation. The data processing pipeline is deployed on a cluster of 3030 cloud instances. Each instance is equipped with 128128 virtual CPUs (vCores), 11 TB of RAM, and 88 NVIDIA L20 GPUs, powered by Intel Xeon Platinum 8575C processors that support vectorized acceleration instructions, including Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). In total, the cluster provides 3,8403,840 vCores, 3030 TB of memory, and 240240 NVIDIA L20 GPUs. Following extensive optimization, the pipeline achieves a daily processing throughput of approximately 200,000200,000 hours of raw audio data.
items.zh: 实现。数据处理流程部署在3030个云实例的集群上。每个实例配备128128个虚拟CPU（vCore）、11 TB RAM和88个NVIDIA L20 GPU，由支持向量化加速指令（包括高级矩阵扩展AMX）的Intel Xeon Platinum 8575C处理器驱动。总体而言，该集群提供3,8403,840个vCore、3030 TB内存和240240个NVIDIA L20 GPU。经过广泛优化，该流程实现了每天约200,000200,000小时原始音频数据的处理吞吐量。

items.en: After the pre-training stage, we perform supervised fine-tuning (SFT) to enhance the performance of Kimi-Audio on instruction following and audio processing. The SFT data can be mainly categorized into three parts: audio understanding, speech conversation, and audio-to-text chat.
items.zh: 预训练阶段之后，我们进行监督微调（SFT）以增强Kimi-Audio在指令遵循和音频处理方面的性能。SFT数据主要分为三类：音频理解、语音对话和音频到文本聊天。

items.en: We mainly leverage open-source datasets for audio understanding. The collected datasets include 66 tasks: Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR), Audio Question Answer (AQA), Automated Audio Caption (AAC), Speech Emotion Recognition (SER), Sound Event Classification (SEC), and Audio Scene Classification (ASC). The details of the datasets and the corresponding training epochs in the SFT stage are shown in Table 1. Besides the open-source datasets, we also utilize 55,00055,000 hours in-house ASR data and 5,2005,200 hours in-house audio data covering the AAC/AQA tasks.
items.zh: 我们主要利用开源数据集进行音频理解。收集的数据集包括66个任务：自动语音识别（ASR）、音频问答（AQA）、自动音频字幕（AAC）、语音情感识别（SER）、声音事件分类（SEC）和音频场景分类（ASC）。数据集详情及SFT阶段相应的训练轮次如表1所示。除了开源数据集，我们还使用了55,00055,000小时的内部ASR数据和5,2005,200小时的内部音频数据，涵盖AAC/AQA任务。

items.en: To activate the Kimi-Audio model’s ability to generate speech with diverse styles and high expressiveness in different conversation scenarios, we construct a large volume of speech conversation data, which consists of multi-turn conversations made up with a series of user queries and assistant responses. For user queries, we instruct LLMs to write the text of user queries and then convert them into speech with our Kimi-TTS system, where the prompt speech is randomly selected from a large timbre set containing more than 125125K timbres. For the assistant responses, we first select a voice actor as our Kimi-Audio speaker and synthesize the assistant responses with appropriate style and emotion with this single timbre. In the following, we introduce the data recording process for the Kimi-Audio speaker, as well as the Kimi-TTS and Kimi-VC systems used to synthesize assistant responses with diverse styles and expressiveness.
items.zh: 为了激活Kimi-Audio模型在不同对话场景中生成具有多样风格和高表现力语音的能力，我们构建了大量语音对话数据，这些数据由一系列用户查询和助手回复组成的多轮对话构成。对于用户查询，我们指示LLM编写用户查询的文本，然后使用我们的Kimi-TTS系统将其转换为语音，其中提示语音从包含超过125125K音色的大型音色集中随机选择。对于助手回复，我们首先选择一位配音演员作为我们的Kimi-Audio说话人，并使用该单一音色合成具有适当风格和情感的助手回复。接下来，我们介绍Kimi-Audio说话人的数据录制过程，以及用于合成具有多样风格和表现力的助手回复的Kimi-TTS和Kimi-VC系统。

items.en: Data Recording for Kimi-Audio Speaker. To achieve diverse and highly expressive styles and emotions in the generated speech, we select a voice actor as the Kimi-Audio speaker and meticulously record a dataset of this speaker in a professional recording studio. We pre-define over 2020 styles and emotions for recording, with each emotion further divided into 55 levels to represent varying emotional intensities. For each style and emotional level, we record an audio as the reference to maintain the consistency of the emotion and style among different text sentences. The whole recording process is guided by a professional recording director.
items.zh: Kimi-Audio说话人的数据录制。为了在生成的语音中实现多样且高度表现力的风格和情感，我们选择一位配音演员作为Kimi-Audio说话人，并在专业录音室中精心录制该说话人的数据集。我们预定义了超过2020种风格和情感进行录制，每种情感进一步分为55个级别以表示不同的情感强度。对于每种风格和情感级别，我们录制一个音频作为参考，以保持不同文本句子之间情感和风格的一致性。整个录制过程由专业录音导演指导。

items.en: Kimi-TTS. We develop a zero-shot text-to-speech synthesis (TTS) system, called Kimi-TTS, to generate speech with only a 33-second prompt, while preserving the timbre, emotion, and style of the prompt speech. With the help of Kimi-TTS, we can synthesize speech for 1) the query text in diverse speakers/timbres with a large timbre set; 2) the response text with the styles and emotions recorded by the Kimi-Audio speaker, a voice actor selected by Kimi. Similar to the architecture of MoonCast [32], Kimi-TTS employs an LLM to generate speech tokens given the prompt speech and input text. Then a flow-matching-based speech detokenizer is used to generate high-quality speech waveforms. We train Kimi-TTS on about 11M hours generated by the automatic data pipeline (Section 3.1) and apply reinforcement learning to further enhance the robustness and quality of the generated speech.
items.zh: Kimi-TTS。我们开发了一个零样本文本到语音合成（TTS）系统，称为Kimi-TTS，仅需33秒的提示即可生成语音，同时保留提示语音的音色、情感和风格。借助Kimi-TTS，我们可以为1）具有大型音色集的多种说话人/音色的查询文本合成语音；2）具有Kimi-Audio说话人（Kimi选择的配音演员）录制的风格和情感的回复文本合成语音。与MoonCast [32]的架构类似，Kimi-TTS使用LLM根据提示语音和输入文本生成语音标记。然后使用基于流匹配的语音解标记器生成高质量语音波形。我们在自动数据流程（第3.1节）生成的约11M小时数据上训练Kimi-TTS，并应用强化学习进一步增强生成语音的鲁棒性和质量。

items.en: Kimi-VC. Since it is difficult for the voice actor to record speech in any styles, emotions, and accents, we develop a voice conversion (VC) system, called Kimi-VC, to convert diverse and in-the-wild speech in different speakers/timbres into the timbre of Kimi-Audio speaker while preserving the styles, emotions, and accents. Built on the Seed-VC framework [46], Kimi-VC incorporates source timbre perturbation via a timbre-shifting model during training, which mitigates information leakage and ensures alignment between training and inference phases. To ensure high quality of voice conversion, we fine-tune the Kimi-VC model using speech data recorded by the Kimi-Audio speaker, a voice actor selected by Kimi.
items.zh: Kimi-VC。由于配音演员难以录制任意风格、情感和口音的语音，我们开发了一个语音转换（VC）系统，称为Kimi-VC，用于将不同说话人/音色中的多样化和野外语音转换为Kimi-Audio说话人的音色，同时保留风格、情感和口音。基于Seed-VC框架[46]，Kimi-VC在训练过程中通过音色偏移模型引入源音色扰动，这减轻了信息泄露并确保了训练和推理阶段之间的一致性。为确保语音转换的高质量，我们使用Kimi-Audio说话人（Kimi选定的配音演员）录制的语音数据对Kimi-VC模型进行微调。

items.en: To help Kimi-Audio with the basic ability on chat, we collect open-source supervised fine-tuning data from text domain, as listed in Table 2, and then convert the user queries to speech with a variety of timbres, resulting in the audio-to-text chat data whose user query is speech while the assistant response is text. Considering that some text cannot be easily converted to speech, we perform several preprocessing steps on text by 1) filtering out text containing complex math, code, table, complex multilingual content, or too long content, 2) making colloquial rewriting, and 3) convert a single-turn question-answer data with complex instruction into multi-turn data with easy and concise instructions.
items.zh: 为了帮助Kimi-Audio具备基本的聊天能力，我们从文本领域收集了开源的监督微调数据，如表2所示，然后将用户查询转换为具有多种音色的语音，从而得到音频到文本的聊天数据，其中用户查询是语音，而助手响应是文本。考虑到某些文本不易转换为语音，我们对文本进行了若干预处理步骤：1）过滤掉包含复杂数学、代码、表格、复杂多语言内容或过长内容的文本；2）进行口语化改写；3）将包含复杂指令的单轮问答数据转换为具有简单简洁指令的多轮数据。

items.en: The pre-training stage of Kimi-Audio aims to learn the knowledge from both the real-world audio and text domains and align them in the model’s latent space, thereby facilitating complex tasks such as audio understanding, audio-to-text chat, and speech conversation. To this end, we design several pre-training tasks with the following aspects: 1) pre-training in the unimodality (i.e., audio and text) to learn the knowledge from each domain individually in Section 4.1.1; 2) learning audio-text mapping in Section 4.1.2; 3) three audio-text interleaving tasks to further bridge two modalities in Section 4.1.3.
items.zh: Kimi-Audio的预训练阶段旨在从真实世界音频和文本领域学习知识，并在模型的潜在空间中对齐它们，从而促进复杂任务，如音频理解、音频到文本聊天和语音对话。为此，我们设计了若干预训练任务，涉及以下方面：1）单模态（即音频和文本）预训练，以分别学习每个领域的知识，详见第4.1.1节；2）学习音频-文本映射，详见第4.1.2节；3）三个音频-文本交错任务，以进一步桥接两种模态，详见第4.1.3节。

items.en: Formally, given a raw audio AA, the data pre-processing pipeline (described in Section 3.1) splits it into a series of segments {S1,S2,…,SN}\{S_{1},S_{2},...,S_{N}\}, and each segment Si,i∈[1,N]S_{i},i\in[1,N] consists of an audio aia_{i} and the corresponding transcription tit_{i}. Furthermore, as illustrated in Section 2.2, for an audio segment aia_{i}, we extract both the continuous acoustic vectors aica_{i}^{c} and the discrete semantic tokens aida_{i}^{d}. To comply with the design of our model architecture in Section 2 which takes discrete semantic audio tokens as the main representation of the input and output, while adding continuous acoustic audio tokens in the input and discrete text tokens in the output, we denote the training sequence as {a1c/a1d/t1,a2c/a2d/t2,…,aNc/aNd/tN}\{a^{c}_{1}/a^{d}_{1}/t_{1},a^{c}_{2}/a^{d}_{2}/t_{2},...,a^{c}_{N}/a^{d}_{N}/t_{N}\}, where aic/aid/tia^{c}_{i}/a^{d}_{i}/t_{i} denotes the semantic audio, acoustic audio, and text sequence for segment ii. We make sure that the audio and text sequences have the same lengths by appending blank tokens to the shorter sequence. The actual pre-training segments can be either one or two of aic/aid/tia^{c}_{i}/a^{d}_{i}/t_{i}, such as aida^{d}_{i}, tit_{i}, aic/aida^{c}_{i}/a^{d}_{i}, or aid/tia^{d}_{i}/t_{i}. For aic/aida^{c}_{i}/a^{d}_{i}, we add the continuous vectors aica_{i}^{c} and the semantic tokens aida_{i}^{d} (the semantic tokens will be converted into embedding using a lookup table) to obtain the final audio feature aia_{i}. Thus, we use aia_{i} to represent aic/aida^{c}_{i}/a^{d}_{i} for short. For aid/tia^{d}_{i}/t_{i}, we add the lookup embedding of the semantic tokens and text tokens as input and generate each token with its respective head, as described in Section 2.
items.zh: 形式上，给定原始音频AA，数据预处理流程（在第3.1节中描述）将其分割为一系列片段{S1,S2,...,SN}\{S_{1},S_{2},...,S_{N}\}，每个片段Si,i∈[1,N]S_{i},i\in[1,N]由音频aia_{i}和对应的转录文本tit_{i}组成。此外，如第2.2节所述，对于音频片段aia_{i}，我们提取连续的声学向量aica_{i}^{c}和离散的语义标记aida_{i}^{d}。为了符合第2节中模型架构的设计，该架构将离散的语义音频标记作为输入和输出的主要表示，同时在输入中添加连续的声学音频标记，在输出中添加离散的文本标记，我们将训练序列表示为{a1c/a1d/t1,a2c/a2d/t2,...,aNc/aNd/tN}\{a^{c}_{1}/a^{d}_{1}/t_{1},a^{c}_{2}/a^{d}_{2}/t_{2},...,a^{c}_{N}/a^{d}_{N}/t_{N}\}，其中aic/aid/tia^{c}_{i}/a^{d}_{i}/t_{i}表示片段ii的语义音频、声学音频和文本序列。我们通过向较短的序列附加空白标记来确保音频和文本序列具有相同的长度。实际的预训练片段可以是aic/aid/tia^{c}_{i}/a^{d}_{i}/t_{i}中的一个或两个，例如aida^{d}_{i}、tit_{i}、aic/aida^{c}_{i}/a^{d}_{i}或aid/tia^{d}_{i}/t_{i}。对于aic/aida^{c}_{i}/a^{d}_{i}，我们将连续向量aica_{i}^{c}和语义标记aida_{i}^{d}（语义标记将使用查找表转换为嵌入）相加，以获得最终的音频特征aia_{i}。因此，我们使用aia_{i}来简写表示aic/aida^{c}_{i}/a^{d}_{i}。对于aid/tia^{d}_{i}/t_{i}，我们将语义标记和文本标记的查找嵌入相加作为输入，并使用各自的头部生成每个标记，如第2节所述。

items.en: With this notation, we formulate the following pre-training tasks in Table 3 and introduce them as follows.
items.zh: 使用这种表示法，我们在表3中制定了以下预训练任务，并介绍如下。

items.en: We first learn the knowledge of text and audio separately. For text pre-training, we directly utilize the text data in MoonLight [44], which is high-quality and comprehensive for training large language models. We apply next-token prediction on text tokens solely. For audio pre-training, for each segment SiS_{i}, we apply next-token prediction on its discrete semantic token sequence aida^{d}_{i}.
items.zh: 我们首先分别学习文本和音频的知识。对于文本预训练，我们直接使用MoonLight [44]中的文本数据，该数据质量高且全面，适用于训练大型语言模型。我们仅对文本标记应用下一个词预测。对于音频预训练，对于每个片段SiS_{i}，我们对其离散语义标记序列aida^{d}_{i}应用下一个词预测。

items.en: Intuitively, in order to align audio and text in a unified space, it is helpful to learn a mapping between two modalities. Thus, we design the automatic speech recognition (ASR) and text-to-speech synthesis (TTS) pre-training tasks. For ASR, we formulate the training sequence as {a1,t1,a2,t2,…,aN,tN}\{a_{1},t_{1},a_{2},t_{2},...,a_{N},t_{N}\}. For TTS, we formulate the training sequence as {t1,a1d,t2,a2d,…,tN,aNd}\{t_{1},a_{1}^{d},t_{2},a_{2}^{d},...,t_{N},a_{N}^{d}\}. We only calculate the loss on text tokens for ASR and on audio semantic tokens for TTS.
items.zh: 直观上，为了在统一空间中对齐音频和文本，学习两种模态之间的映射是有帮助的。因此，我们设计了自动语音识别（ASR）和文本到语音合成（TTS）预训练任务。对于ASR，我们将训练序列表示为{a1,t1,a2,t2,...,aN,tN}\{a_{1},t_{1},a_{2},t_{2},...,a_{N},t_{N}\}。对于TTS，我们将训练序列表示为{t1,a1d,t2,a2d,...,tN,aNd}\{t_{1},a_{1}^{d},t_{2},a_{2}^{d},...,t_{N},a_{N}^{d}\}。我们仅对ASR的文本标记和TTS的音频语义标记计算损失。

items.en: To further bridge the gap between audio and text modalities, we design three audio-text interleaving pre-training tasks.
items.zh: 为了进一步弥合音频和文本模态之间的差距，我们设计了三个音频-文本交错预训练任务。

items.en: Audio to semantic token interleaving. We formulate the training sequence as {a1,a2d,a3,a4d,…,aN−1,aNd}\{a_{1},a_{2}^{d},a_{3},a_{4}^{d},...,a_{N-1},a_{N}^{d}\}444It is also possible that the first segment is a1da_{1}^{d}, or the last segment is aNa_{N}.. Then we only calculate the loss on the semantic audio tokens aida_{i}^{d}, but not on ai−1a_{i-1}.
items.zh: 音频到语义标记交错。我们将训练序列表示为{a1,a2d,a3,a4d,...,aN−1,aNd}\{a_{1},a_{2}^{d},a_{3},a_{4}^{d},...,a_{N-1},a_{N}^{d}\}444第一个片段也可能是a1da_{1}^{d}，或者最后一个片段是aNa_{N}。然后我们仅对语义音频标记aida_{i}^{d}计算损失，而不对ai−1a_{i-1}计算损失。

items.en: Audio to text interleaving. We formulate the training sequence as {a1,t2,a3,t4,…,aN−1,tN}\{a_{1},t_{2},a_{3},t_{4},...,a_{N-1},t_{N}\}. We only calculate the loss on text tokens tit_{i}.
items.zh: 音频到文本交错。我们将训练序列表示为{a1,t2,a3,t4,...,aN−1,tN}\{a_{1},t_{2},a_{3},t_{4},...,a_{N-1},t_{N}\}。我们仅对文本标记tit_{i}计算损失。

items.en: Audio to semantic token + text interleaving. We formulate the training sequence as {a1,a2d/t2,a3,a4d/t4,…,aN−1,aNd/tN}\{a_{1},a_{2}^{d}/t_{2},a_{3},a_{4}^{d}/t_{4},...,a_{N-1},a_{N}^{d}/t_{N}\}. For aid/tia_{i}^{d}/t_{i}, as the semantic audio token sequence is always longer than the text token sequence, the prediction of the semantic token is like a streaming text-to-speech task as in Section 4.1.2. Empirically, we find that the prediction of the first few semantic tokens is hard because the model needs to concurrently predict the next text token and its semantic audio token. We address this issue by delaying the prediction of the first several semantic audio tokens by prepending 66 special blank tokens (66 is determined by trading off the generation quality and latency according to preliminary experiments) to the semantic audio tokens.
items.zh: 音频到语义标记+文本交错。我们将训练序列表示为{a1,a2d/t2,a3,a4d/t4,...,aN−1,aNd/tN}\{a_{1},a_{2}^{d}/t_{2},a_{3},a_{4}^{d}/t_{4},...,a_{N-1},a_{N}^{d}/t_{N}\}。对于aid/tia_{i}^{d}/t_{i}，由于语义音频标记序列总是比文本标记序列长，语义标记的预测类似于第4.1.2节中的流式文本到语音任务。根据经验，我们发现前几个语义标记的预测很困难，因为模型需要同时预测下一个文本标记及其语义音频标记。我们通过将前几个语义音频标记的预测延迟来解决这个问题，即在语义音频标记前添加66个特殊空白标记（66是根据初步实验在生成质量和延迟之间权衡确定的）。

items.en: We initialize the audio LLM of Kimi-Audio from the pre-trained Qwen2.5 7B model [76] and extend its vocabulary with semantic audio tokens and special tokens. We perform pre-training on the above pre-training tasks with the corresponding task weight 1:7:1:1:1:1:21:7:1:1:1:1:2, as shown in Table 3. We pre-train Kimi-Audio using 585585B audio tokens and 585585B text tokens with 11 epoch. We use AdamW [48] optimizer with a learning rate schedule from 2​e−52e^{-5} to 2​e−62e^{-6} in a cosine decay. We use 11% tokens for learning rate warmup.
items.zh: 我们从预训练的Qwen2.5 7B模型[76]初始化Kimi-Audio的音频LLM，并扩展其词汇表以包含语义音频标记和特殊标记。我们在上述预训练任务上执行预训练，对应的任务权重为1:7:1:1:1:1:21:7:1:1:1:1:2，如表3所示。我们使用585585B音频标记和585585B文本标记对Kimi-Audio进行预训练，共1个周期。我们使用AdamW [48]优化器，学习率从2e−52e^{-5}到2e−62e^{-6}按余弦衰减。我们使用11%的标记进行学习率预热。

items.en: The continuous acoustic feature extraction module in audio tokenizer is initialized from Whisper large-v3 [58], which can capture the fine-grained acoustic characteristics inherent in the input audio signal. During the initial phases of model pretraining (about 2020% tokens in pretraining), the parameters of this whisper-based feature extractor are kept frozen. Subsequently, the feature extractor is unfrozen, enabling its parameters to be fine-tuned jointly with the rest of the model, allowing it to adapt more specifically to the nuances of the training data and the requirements of the target tasks.
items.zh: 音频分词器中的连续声学特征提取模块从Whisper large-v3 [58]初始化，该模块能够捕获输入音频信号中固有的细粒度声学特征。在模型预训练的初始阶段（约占预训练标记的2020%），该基于Whisper的特征提取器的参数保持冻结。随后，特征提取器被解冻，使其参数能够与模型其余部分一起微调，从而使其更具体地适应训练数据的细微差别和目标任务的要求。

items.en: After pre-training Kimi-Audio with massive real-world audio and text data, we perform supervised fine-tuning to equip it with the ability of instruction-following. We have the following design choices: 1) considering the downstream tasks are diverse, we do not set special task switching operations, but use natural language as instructions for each task; 2) for instruction, we construct both the audio and text versions (i.e., the audio is generated by Kimi-TTS in a zero-shot way given the text) and randomly choose one during training; 3) to enhance the robustness of instruction-following capability, we construct 200200 instructions for ASR task and 3030 instructions for other tasks by LLM and randomly choose one for each training sample. As described in Section 3.2, we build about 300300K hours of data for supervised fine-tuning.
items.zh: 在使用大量真实世界音频和文本数据预训练Kimi-Audio之后，我们执行监督微调以使其具备指令跟随能力。我们有以下设计选择：1）考虑到下游任务的多样性，我们不设置特殊的任务切换操作，而是使用自然语言作为每个任务的指令；2）对于指令，我们构建音频和文本版本（即音频由Kimi-TTS以零样本方式根据文本生成），并在训练期间随机选择其中一个；3）为了增强指令跟随能力的鲁棒性，我们为ASR任务构建了200200条指令，为其他任务构建了3030条指令（由LLM生成），并为每个训练样本随机选择一条。如第3.2节所述，我们构建了约300300K小时的监督微调数据。

items.en: As shown in Table 1 and Table 2, we fine-tune Kimi-Audio on each data source with 22-44 epochs based on comprehensive ablation experiments. We use AdamW [48] optimizer with a learning rate schedule from 1​e−51e^{-5} to 1​e−61e^{-6} in a cosine decay. We use 1010% tokens for learning rate warmup.
items.zh: 如表1和表2所示，基于全面的消融实验，我们在每个数据源上对Kimi-Audio进行22-44个周期的微调。我们使用AdamW [48]优化器，学习率从1e−51e^{-5}到1e−61e^{-6}按余弦衰减。我们使用1010%的标记进行学习率预热。

items.en: We train the audio detokenizer in three stages. Firstly, we use about 11M hours of audio from the pre-training data described in Section 3.1 and pre-train both the flow-matching model and the vocoder to learn audio with diverse timbre, prosody, and quality. Secondly, we adopt the chunk-wise fine-tuning strategy with a dynamic chunk size from 0.50.5 seconds to 33 seconds on the same pre-training data [32]. Finally, we fine-tune on the high-quality single-speaker recording data from the Kimi-Audio speaker.
items.zh: 我们分三个阶段训练音频解分词器。首先，我们使用来自第3.1节描述的预训练数据中约11M小时的音频，预训练流匹配模型和声码器，以学习具有多样音色、韵律和质量的音频。其次，我们在相同的预训练数据上采用分块微调策略，动态块大小从0.50.5秒到33秒[32]。最后，我们在来自Kimi-Audio说话人的高质量单说话人录音数据上进行微调。

items.en: Kimi-Audio is designed to handle various audio-related tasks, such as speech recognition, audio understanding, audio-to-text chat, and speech-to-speech conversation. We take real-time speech-to-speech conversation as an example to illustrate the practices in Kimi-Audio deployment, since this task is more complicated than the rest of audio tasks in terms of infrastructure and engineering efforts. We first introduce the workflow of real-time speech conversation between the client (e.g., Kimi APP or web browser) and the server (Kimi-Audio service) and then describe the practices of product deployment.
items.zh: Kimi-Audio旨在处理各种音频相关任务，如语音识别、音频理解、音频到文本聊天和语音到语音对话。我们以实时语音到语音对话为例，说明Kimi-Audio部署中的实践，因为该任务在基础设施和工程工作方面比其他音频任务更复杂。我们首先介绍客户端（例如Kimi APP或Web浏览器）和服务器（Kimi-Audio服务）之间实时语音对话的工作流程，然后描述产品部署的实践。

items.capEn: Figure 4: The client-server communication for real-time speech-to-speech conversation in Kimi-Audio.
items.capZh: 图4：Kimi-Audio中实时语音到语音对话的客户端-服务器通信。

items.en: The workflow of a speech-to-speech conversation between the user client (e.g., Kimi APP) and the server (Kimi-Audio service) is illustrated in Figure 4. The workflow proceeds in the following manner for each conversation round:
items.zh: 用户客户端（例如Kimi APP）和服务器（Kimi-Audio服务）之间的语音到语音对话工作流程如图4所示。每个对话轮次的工作流程如下：

items.en: The user speaks to the client (e.g., Kimi APP or web browser), and the audio data is collected and streamed to the server.
items.zh: 用户对客户端（例如Kimi APP或Web浏览器）说话，音频数据被收集并流式传输到服务器。

items.en: On the server side, a voice activity detection (VAD) module determines if the user has finished speaking.
items.zh: 在服务器端，语音活动检测（VAD）模块判断用户是否已结束说话。

items.en: Once the user stops speaking, the server sends a commit signal and initiates the inference process of the Kimi-Audio model.
items.zh: 一旦用户停止说话，服务器发送提交信号并启动Kimi-Audio模型的推理过程。

items.en: During inference, the client receives audio chunks in real-time as they are generated and starts playing them for the user.
items.zh: 推理过程中，客户端实时接收生成的音频块并开始为用户播放。

items.en: The client (mobile phone or web browser) plays the received audio chunks back to the user.
items.zh: 客户端（手机或网页浏览器）将接收到的音频块回放给用户。

items.en: The inference process of Kimi-Audio on the server side for each round follows these steps. First, the input audio is converted to discrete semantic tokens and continuous acoustic vectors using the audio tokenizer. Next, the input to the Audio LLM is assembled by concatenating the system prompt tokens, audio tokens, and conversation history tokens. The token sequence is then passed to the Audio LLM, which generates output tokens. Finally, the output tokens are converted back into an audio waveform using the detokenizer.
items.zh: 每轮对话中，服务器端Kimi-Audio的推理过程遵循以下步骤。首先，使用音频分词器将输入音频转换为离散语义标记和连续声学向量。接着，通过拼接系统提示标记、音频标记和对话历史标记来组装Audio LLM的输入。然后，将标记序列传递给Audio LLM，由其生成输出标记。最后，使用解分词器将输出标记转换回音频波形。

items.capEn: Figure 5: The workflow of production deployment for real-time speech-to-speech conversation in Kimi-Audio.
items.capZh: 图5：Kimi-Audio中实时语音到语音对话的生产部署工作流程。

items.en: As shown in Figure 5, in a production environment, all core components: Audio Tokenizer, Audio LLM, and Audio Detokenizer, are computationally intensive, requiring a scalable and efficient infrastructure. To address this, we designed the production deployment architecture as follows.
items.zh: 如图5所示，在生产环境中，所有核心组件（音频分词器、Audio LLM和音频解分词器）都是计算密集型的，需要可扩展且高效的底层架构。为此，我们设计了如下生产部署架构。

items.en: Kimi-Audio RTC Service. This service interfaces with the client, receiving audio from the user, forwarding it to the Inference Scheduler, and returning the generated audio chunks to the client. We use the WebRTC protocol to ensure a stable and low-latency connection.
items.zh: Kimi-Audio RTC服务。该服务与客户端交互，接收用户音频，将其转发给推理调度器，并将生成的音频块返回给客户端。我们使用WebRTC协议确保稳定且低延迟的连接。

items.en: Inference Scheduler. The Inference Scheduler manages the conversation flow by maintaining conversation history as tokens in a storage backend. For each interaction round, it performs the following steps:
items.zh: 推理调度器。推理调度器通过在后端存储中维护对话历史（以标记形式）来管理对话流程。对于每轮交互，它执行以下步骤：

items.en: Call the Tokenizer Service to convert the user’s audio into tokens.
items.zh: 调用分词器服务将用户音频转换为标记。

items.en: Construct the model input by combining the new tokens with the conversation history.
items.zh: 通过将新标记与对话历史组合来构建模型输入。

items.en: Send the input to the LLM Service to generate response tokens.
items.zh: 将输入发送给LLM服务以生成响应标记。

items.en: Call the Detokenizer Service to convert the response tokens into audio output.
items.zh: 调用解分词器服务将响应标记转换为音频输出。

items.en: Additionally, it stores all output tokens as part of the ongoing conversation history to ensure continuity in the dialogue.
items.zh: 此外，它将所有输出标记存储为持续对话历史的一部分，以确保对话的连续性。

items.en: Tokenizer/Detokenizer/LLM Services: These services handle model inference and are equipped with a load balancer and multiple inference instances to handle requests in parallel, ensuring scalability.
items.zh: 分词器/解分词器/LLM服务：这些服务处理模型推理，并配备负载均衡器和多个推理实例以并行处理请求，确保可扩展性。

items.en: This modular architecture ensures that Kimi-Audio can scale effectively to meet the performance demands of real-time speech interactions while maintaining low latency and high availability in production.
items.zh: 这种模块化架构确保Kimi-Audio能够有效扩展，满足实时语音交互的性能需求，同时在生产环境中保持低延迟和高可用性。

items.en: Evaluating audio foundation models and comparing with previous state-of-the-art systems are challenging, due to some inherent issues in the audio community. Thus, we first develop a fair, reproducible, and comprehensive evaluation toolkit for audio foundation models in Section 6.1, and then evaluate Kimi-Audio on a variety of audio processing tasks including speech recognition, general audio understanding, audio-to-text chat, and speech conversation, and compare Kimi-Audio with previous systems to demonstrate the advantages in Section 6.2.
items.zh: 评估音频基础模型并与先前最先进系统进行比较具有挑战性，因为音频社区存在一些固有问题。因此，我们首先在第6.1节开发了一个公平、可重复且全面的音频基础模型评估工具包，然后在第6.2节中评估Kimi-Audio在多种音频处理任务（包括语音识别、通用音频理解、音频到文本聊天和语音对话）上的表现，并与先前系统进行比较以展示其优势。

items.en: Even if an audio foundation model is fully open-source, it is still troublesome to reproduce the same results as reported in its paper or technical report, let alone those closed-source models. We analyze the challenges in evaluating and comparing audio foundation models in various audio processing tasks as follows:
items.zh: 即使音频基础模型完全开源，重现其论文或技术报告中报告的结果仍然很麻烦，更不用说那些闭源模型了。我们分析了在各种音频处理任务中评估和比较音频基础模型面临的挑战如下：

items.en: Limitations in Metrics. Current practices suffer from inconsistent metric implementations (e.g., variations in Word Error Rate calculation due to different text normalizations) and inadequate assessment methods (e.g., relying solely on exact string matching for tasks like audio question answering fails to capture the semantic correctness of complex LLM responses).
items.zh: 指标局限性。当前实践存在指标实现不一致（例如，由于文本归一化不同导致的词错误率计算差异）和评估方法不充分（例如，仅依赖精确字符串匹配进行音频问答等任务无法捕捉复杂LLM响应的语义正确性）的问题。

items.en: Diverse Configurations. Reproducibility is severely hampered by the high sensitivity of model performance to inference parameters such as decoding temperature, system prompts, and task prompts.
items.zh: 配置多样性。模型性能对推理参数（如解码温度、系统提示和任务提示）的高度敏感性严重阻碍了可重复性。

items.en: Lack of Generation Evaluation While progress has been made in understanding tasks, assessing the quality and coherence of the generated audio response still lacks benchmarks.
items.zh: 缺乏生成评估：尽管在理解任务方面取得了进展，但评估生成音频响应的质量和连贯性仍缺乏基准。

items.en: To address these critical limitations, we develop an open-source evaluation toolkit for audio foundation models on audio understanding, generation, and conversation tasks. It currently integrates and supports Kimi-Audio and a series of recent audio LLMs [11, 74, 84, 41, 28], and can be leveraged to evaluate any other audio foundation models. The toolkit provides the following features and benefits:
items.zh: 为解决这些关键限制，我们开发了一个用于音频基础模型在音频理解、生成和对话任务上的开源评估工具包。它目前集成并支持Kimi-Audio以及一系列最新的音频大语言模型[11, 74, 84, 41, 28]，并可用于评估任何其他音频基础模型。该工具包提供以下特性和优势：

items.en: We implement a standardized WER calculation (based on Qwen-2-Audio [11]) and integrate GPT-4o-mini as an intelligent judge (following [8]) for tasks like audio question answering. This approach overcomes the limitations of inconsistent metrics and simplistic string matching, enabling fair comparison.
items.zh: 我们实现了标准化的WER计算（基于Qwen-2-Audio [11]），并集成了GPT-4o-mini作为智能评判者（遵循[8]），用于音频问答等任务。这种方法克服了指标不一致和简单字符串匹配的局限性，实现了公平比较。

items.en: Our toolkit offers a single unified platform supporting diverse models and versions, simplifying side-by-side comparisons. It provides a crucial structure for defining and sharing standardized inference parameters and prompting strategies (“recipes”), thereby directly addressing inconsistencies in evaluation setups and fostering greater reproducibility across different research works.
items.zh: 我们的工具包提供了一个统一的平台，支持多种模型和版本，简化了并排比较。它为定义和共享标准化推理参数和提示策略（“配方”）提供了关键结构，从而直接解决了评估设置中的不一致问题，并促进了不同研究工作之间的可重复性。

items.en: We record and release an evaluation benchmark to test the ability of audio LLMs on speech conversation from the perspective of 1) speech control on emotion, speed, and accent; 2) empathy conversation; and 3) diverse styles such as storytelling and tongue twister.
items.zh: 我们记录并发布了一个评估基准，用于从以下角度测试音频大语言模型在语音对话方面的能力：1）情感、语速和口音的语音控制；2）共情对话；以及3）多样化的风格，如讲故事和绕口令。

items.en: We open-source this toolkit to the community https://github.com/MoonshotAI/Kimi-Audio-Evalkit. We believe this toolkit can serve as a valuable asset to advance the field by promoting more reliable and comparable benchmarking. We actively encourage researchers and developers to utilize it, contribute by adding new models and datasets, and help refine standardized evaluation protocols and inference recipes. In this way, we can build a better ecosystem for the audio community.
items.zh: 我们将此工具包开源至社区 https://github.com/MoonshotAI/Kimi-Audio-Evalkit。我们相信，该工具包通过促进更可靠和可比较的基准测试，能够成为推动该领域发展的宝贵资产。我们积极鼓励研究人员和开发者使用它，通过添加新模型和数据集做出贡献，并帮助完善标准化评估协议和推理配方。通过这种方式，我们可以为音频社区构建一个更好的生态系统。

items.en: In this section, based on our evaluation toolkit, we detail the evaluation of Kimi-Audio across a comprehensive suite of audio processing tasks, including Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR), audio understanding, audio-to-text chat, and speech conversation. We compare Kimi-Audio against other audio foundation models (Qwen2-Audio [11], Baichuan-Audio [41], Step-Audio [28], GLM-4-Voice [84], and Qwen2.5-Omini [73]) using established benchmarks and internal test sets.
items.zh: 在本节中，基于我们的评估工具包，我们详细评估了Kimi-Audio在一系列音频处理任务上的表现，包括自动语音识别（ASR）、音频理解、音频到文本聊天和语音对话。我们使用已建立的基准和内部测试集，将Kimi-Audio与其他音频基础模型（Qwen2-Audio [11]、Baichuan-Audio [41]、Step-Audio [28]、GLM-4-Voice [84]和Qwen2.5-Omni [73]）进行了比较。

items.en: The ASR capabilities of Kimi-Audio were evaluated on diverse datasets spanning multiple languages and acoustic conditions. As presented in Table 4, Kimi-Audio consistently demonstrates superior performance compared to previous models. We report Word Error Rate (WER) on these datasets, where lower values indicate better performance.
items.zh: Kimi-Audio的ASR能力在涵盖多种语言和声学条件的多样化数据集上进行了评估。如表4所示，与之前的模型相比，Kimi-Audio始终表现出优越的性能。我们报告了这些数据集上的词错误率（WER），数值越低表示性能越好。

items.en: Notably, Kimi-Audio achieves the best results on the widely-used LibriSpeech [54] benchmark, attaining error rates of 1.281.28 on test-clean and 2.422.42 on test-other, significantly outperforming models like Qwen2-Audio-base and Qwen2.5-Omni. For Mandarin ASR benchmarks, Kimi-Audio sets SOTA results on AISHELL-1 [4] (0.600.60) and AISHELL-2 ios [17] (2.562.56). Furthermore, it excels on the challenging WenetSpeech [85] dataset, achieving the lowest error rates on both test-meeting and test-net. Finally, evaluation on our internal Kimi-ASR test set confirms the model robustness. These results demonstrate the strong ASR capabilities of Kimi-Audio across various domains and languages.
items.zh: 值得注意的是，Kimi-Audio在广泛使用的LibriSpeech [54]基准上取得了最佳结果，在test-clean上错误率为1.28，在test-other上错误率为2.42，显著优于Qwen2-Audio-base和Qwen2.5-Omni等模型。对于中文ASR基准，Kimi-Audio在AISHELL-1 [4]（0.60）和AISHELL-2 ios [17]（2.56）上取得了SOTA结果。此外，它在具有挑战性的WenetSpeech [85]数据集上表现出色，在test-meeting和test-net上均取得了最低错误率。最后，在我们内部的Kimi-ASR测试集上的评估证实了模型的鲁棒性。这些结果证明了Kimi-Audio在不同领域和语言上的强大ASR能力。

items.en: Beyond speech recognition, we assess Kimi-Audio’s ability to comprehend diverse audio signals, including music, sound events, and speech. Table 5 summarizes the performance on various audio understanding benchmarks, where higher scores generally indicate better performance.
items.zh: 除了语音识别，我们还评估了Kimi-Audio理解多样化音频信号的能力，包括音乐、声音事件和语音。表5总结了在各种音频理解基准上的性能，分数越高通常表示性能越好。

items.en: On the MMAU benchmark [60], Kimi-Audio demonstrates superior understanding across sound category (73.2773.27), and speech category (60.6660.66). Similarly, it outperforms other models on the MELD [56] speech emotion understanding task, scoring 59.1359.13. Kimi-Audio also leads on tasks involving non-speech sound classification (VocalSound [23] and Nonspeech7k [59]) and acoustic scene classification (TUT2017 [53] and CochlScene [31]). These results highlight Kimi-Audio’s advanced capabilities in interpreting complex acoustic information beyond simple speech recognition.
items.zh: 在MMAU基准[60]上，Kimi-Audio在声音类别（73.27）和语音类别（60.66）上表现出优越的理解能力。类似地，它在MELD [56]语音情感理解任务上优于其他模型，得分为59.13。Kimi-Audio还在非语音声音分类（VocalSound [23]和Nonspeech7k [59]）和声学场景分类（TUT2017 [53]和CochlScene [31]）任务上领先。这些结果突显了Kimi-Audio在解释超出简单语音识别的复杂声学信息方面的先进能力。

items.en: We evaluate the ability of Kimi-Audio to engage in text conversations based on audio input using the OpenAudioBench [41] and VoiceBench benchmarks [8]. These benchmarks assess various aspects like instruction following, question answering, and reasoning. Performance metrics are benchmark-specific, with higher scores indicating better conversational ability. The results are presented in Table 6.
items.zh: 我们使用OpenAudioBench [41]和VoiceBench基准[8]评估了Kimi-Audio基于音频输入进行文本对话的能力。这些基准评估了指令遵循、问答和推理等多个方面。性能指标因基准而异，分数越高表示对话能力越强。结果如表6所示。

items.en: On OpenAudioBench, Kimi-Audio achieves state-of-the-art performance on several sub-tasks, including AlpacaEval, Llama Questions, and TriviaQA, and achieves highly competitive performance on Reasoning QA and Web Questions.
items.zh: 在OpenAudioBench上，Kimi-Audio在多个子任务上取得了最先进的性能，包括AlpacaEval、Llama Questions和TriviaQA，并在推理问答和Web Questions上取得了极具竞争力的性能。

items.en: The VoiceBench evaluation further confirms Kimi-Audio’s strengths. It consistently outperforms all compared models on AlpacaEval (4.464.46), CommonEval (3.973.97), SD-QA (63.1263.12), MMSU (62.1762.17), OpenBookQA (83.5283.52), Advbench (100.00100.00), and IFEval (61.1061.10). Kimi-Audio’s overall performance across these comprehensive benchmarks demonstrates its superior ability in audio-based conversation and complex reasoning tasks.
items.zh: VoiceBench评估进一步证实了Kimi-Audio的优势。它在AlpacaEval（4.46）、CommonEval（3.97）、SD-QA（63.12）、MMSU（62.17）、OpenBookQA（83.52）、Advbench（100.00）和IFEval（61.10）上始终优于所有比较的模型。Kimi-Audio在这些综合基准上的整体表现证明了其在基于音频的对话和复杂推理任务中的卓越能力。

items.en: Finally, we assess the end-to-end speech conversation capabilities of Kimi-Audio based on subjective evaluations across multiple dimensions. As shown in Table 7, Kimi-Audio was compared against models like GPT-4o and GLM-4-Voice based on human ratings (on a 11-55 scale, higher is better).
items.zh: 最后，我们基于多个维度的主观评估评估了Kimi-Audio的端到端语音对话能力。如表7所示，Kimi-Audio与GPT-4o和GLM-4-Voice等模型基于人类评分（1-5分制，分数越高越好）进行了比较。

items.en: Excluding GPT-4o, Kimi-Audio achieves the highest scores for emotion control, empathy, and speed control. While GLM-4-Voice shows slightly better accent control, Kimi-Audio achieves a strong overall average score of 3.903.90. This score is higher than Step-Audio-chat (3.333.33), GPT-4o-mini (3.453.45), and GLM-4-Voice (3.653.65), and remains a small margin with GPT-4o (4.064.06). Overall, the evaluation results demonstrate Kimi-Audio’s proficiency in generating expressive and controllable speech.
items.zh: 排除GPT-4o，Kimi-Audio在情感控制、共情和语速控制方面获得了最高分。虽然GLM-4-Voice在口音控制上略胜一筹，但Kimi-Audio获得了3.90的总体平均分，高于Step-Audio-chat（3.33）、GPT-4o-mini（3.45）和GLM-4-Voice（3.65），与GPT-4o（4.06）的差距很小。总体而言，评估结果表明Kimi-Audio在生成富有表现力和可控的语音方面具有熟练能力。

items.en: The application of large language models (LLMs) to audio tasks has led to remarkable progress across a wide range of domains, including automatic speech recognition (ASR), audio understanding, text-to-speech synthesis (TTS), general audio generation, and speech-based human-computer interaction. These efforts explore how to bridge the gap between raw acoustic signals and linguistic reasoning by treating audio as a tokenizable sequence, enabling LLMs to process or generate audio in a language-like manner.
items.zh: 将大语言模型（LLM）应用于音频任务已在广泛领域取得了显著进展，包括自动语音识别（ASR）、音频理解、文本到语音合成（TTS）、通用音频生成以及基于语音的人机交互。这些工作通过将音频视为可标记化的序列，探索如何弥合原始声学信号与语言推理之间的差距，使大语言模型能够以类似语言的方式处理或生成音频。

items.en: A number of LLM-based systems have been developed to improve automatic speech recognition (ASR) and broader audio understanding tasks. Whisper [58] serves as a powerful audio encoder, and when combined with large language models (LLMs), it significantly enhances the performance of speech understanding systems. This approach has been successfully utilized in models such as Qwen-Audio [10], Qwen2-Audio [11], SALMONN [63], and OSUM [21] These systems, however, are mostly limited to understanding tasks and do not natively support audio output.
items.zh: 已经开发了许多基于大语言模型的系统来改进自动语音识别（ASR）和更广泛的音频理解任务。Whisper [58] 作为一个强大的音频编码器，当与大语言模型（LLM）结合时，显著增强了语音理解系统的性能。这种方法已成功应用于Qwen-Audio [10]、Qwen2-Audio [11]、SALMONN [63]和OSUM [21]等模型中。然而，这些系统大多局限于理解任务，并不原生支持音频输出。

items.en: For speech synthesis and general audio generation, models such as AudioLM [3], VALL-E [70], and LLASA [80] tokenize audio via neural codecs and use decoder-only language models for autoregressive generation. Other efforts like UniAudio [77] and VoiceBox [37] extend these methods with hybrid tokenization or flow matching to improve quality and control. While these models can produce high-fidelity audio, they typically focus on generation only and lack understanding and conversation capabilities or instruction-following speech interaction.
items.zh: 对于语音合成和通用音频生成，诸如AudioLM [3]、VALL-E [70]和LLASA [80]等模型通过神经编解码器对音频进行标记化，并使用仅解码器语言模型进行自回归生成。其他工作如UniAudio [77]和VoiceBox [37]通过混合标记化或流匹配扩展了这些方法，以提高质量和可控性。虽然这些模型可以生成高保真音频，但它们通常只专注于生成，缺乏理解和对话能力或指令遵循的语音交互。

items.en: Recent models have moved toward enabling real-time, end-to-end speech interaction. Moshi [14], GLM-4-Voice [84], and Mini-Omni [72] adopt interleaved or parallel decoding to support simultaneous generation of text and audio tokens, facilitating low-latency dialogue systems. OmniFlatten [86] introduces a progressive training pipeline to adapt a frozen LLM for full-duplex conversation. LLaMA-Omni [18] and Freeze-Omni [71] further refine duplex speech interaction through streaming decoders or multi-task alignment strategies. However, these systems often rely heavily on speech-only datasets and compromise language modeling quality or generality due to limited pre-training.
items.zh: 最近的模型已转向支持实时、端到端的语音交互。Moshi [14]、GLM-4-Voice [84]和Mini-Omni [72]采用交错或并行解码来支持文本和音频令牌的同时生成，从而促进低延迟对话系统。OmniFlatten [86]引入了一种渐进式训练流程，使冻结的大语言模型适应全双工对话。LLaMA-Omni [18]和Freeze-Omni [71]通过流式解码器或多任务对齐策略进一步优化了双工语音交互。然而，这些系统通常严重依赖纯语音数据集，并且由于预训练有限，会损害语言建模质量或通用性。

items.en: A small number of recent works aim to unify understanding and generation within a single multimodal model. Baichuan-Audio [41] uses a multi-codebook discretization to capture both semantic and acoustic features, enabling real-time interaction and strong question-answering capabilities. However, its focus on speech domain limits its broader applicability, especially for non-speech audio tasks like music or environmental sound. Step-Audio [28], on the other hand, provides a powerful solution for real-time speech interaction with a 130B-parameter unified speech-text multimodal model. While Step-Audio demonstrates strong performance, its dependency on synthetic voice data generation and the high computational costs associated with its 130B parameters pose significant barriers to accessibility and cost-effectiveness for a broader user base. Qwen2.5-Omni [74] introduces a Thinker-Talker architecture for simultaneous text and speech decoding and achieves strong benchmark results, but its design primarily emphasizes streaming inference, and it lacks an extensive pre-training phase on raw audio.
items.zh: 近期少量工作旨在将理解与生成统一到单一多模态模型中。Baichuan-Audio [41] 使用多码本离散化来捕获语义和声学特征，实现实时交互和强大的问答能力。然而，其对语音领域的关注限制了其更广泛的适用性，尤其是对于音乐或环境声音等非语音音频任务。另一方面，Step-Audio [28] 通过一个130B参数的统一语音-文本多模态模型，为实时语音交互提供了强大的解决方案。虽然Step-Audio表现出强劲性能，但其对合成语音数据生成的依赖以及130B参数带来的高计算成本，对更广泛用户群的可及性和成本效益构成了重大障碍。Qwen2.5-Omni [74] 引入了Thinker-Talker架构用于同时的文本和语音解码，并在基准测试中取得了强劲结果，但其设计主要强调流式推理，并且缺乏在原始音频上的大规模预训练阶段。

items.en: Kimi-Audio advances beyond these limitations by introducing a truly universal and open-source audio foundation model that supports speech recognition, audio understanding, audio generation, and speech conversation in a single framework. It adopts a hybrid input representation combining Whisper-derived continuous acoustic features and discrete semantic tokens (12.512.5Hz), ensuring rich perception and efficient modeling. The audio LLM is initialized from a text LLM, with dual-generation heads for text and audio, and a chunk-wise flow-matching detokenizer paired with BigVGAN [38] to produce expressive and low-latency speech.
items.zh: Kimi-Audio 通过引入一个真正通用且开源的基础音频模型，在单一框架内支持语音识别、音频理解、音频生成和语音对话，从而超越了这些限制。它采用混合输入表示，结合了Whisper衍生的连续声学特征和离散语义标记（12.5Hz），确保了丰富的感知和高效的建模。音频LLM从文本LLM初始化，具有用于文本和音频的双生成头，以及一个分块流匹配去分词器与BigVGAN [38] 配对，以生成富有表现力和低延迟的语音。

items.en: Most critically, Kimi-Audio features extensive multimodal pretraining on 1313 million hours of curated audio data across speech, music, and environmental sound—a scale far exceeding prior works. The pretraining tasks include audio-only, text-only, audio-to-text, and interleaved modalities, which enable the model to learn generalizable audio reasoning and maintain strong language abilities. This is followed by instruction-based fine-tuning across diverse tasks, leading to state-of-the-art results on ASR, general audio understanding, audio-text chat, and speech conversation benchmarks.
items.zh: 最关键的是，Kimi-Audio 在1313万小时的精选音频数据（涵盖语音、音乐和环境声音）上进行了大规模多模态预训练——这一规模远超先前的工作。预训练任务包括纯音频、纯文本、音频到文本以及交错模态，这使得模型能够学习可泛化的音频推理并保持强大的语言能力。随后是针对不同任务的基于指令的微调，在ASR、通用音频理解、音频文本聊天和语音对话基准测试中取得了最先进的结果。

items.en: In contrast to existing models that are either limited in scope, lack pre-training, or are not publicly available, Kimi-Audio is a fully open-source, pre-trained, instruction-followable, and real-time capable model. Its comprehensive coverage, scalable architecture, and broad task alignment make it a significant step toward general-purpose audio intelligence.
items.zh: 与现有模型（要么范围有限、缺乏预训练，要么未公开）相比，Kimi-Audio 是一个完全开源、预训练、可遵循指令且支持实时的模型。其全面的覆盖范围、可扩展的架构和广泛的任务对齐，使其成为迈向通用音频智能的重要一步。

items.en: Although Kimi-Audio has achieved significant advancements in building universal audio foundation models, several challenges remain in the quest for more capable and intelligent audio processing systems. We describe the challenges and point out several exciting future directions as follows.
items.zh: 尽管Kimi-Audio在构建通用音频基础模型方面取得了显著进展，但在追求更强大、更智能的音频处理系统方面仍存在若干挑战。我们描述了这些挑战，并指出了以下几个令人兴奋的未来方向。

items.en: From Audio Transcription to Audio Description. Current pre-training paradigms for audio foundation models typically leverage audio-text pre-training to bridge the gap between text and audio, where the text is obtained from audio (speech) by ASR transcription. However, text transcription focuses on the content of spoken words (what is said), neglecting important information in audio, such as paralanguage information (e.g., emotion, style, timbre, tone), acoustic scene, and non-linguistic sounds. Thus, it is important to introduce descriptive text (i.e., audio caption) to depict the audio in richer context. Incorporating both transcriptive and descriptive text of the audio enables models to better understand and generate not only spoken language but also complex acoustic environments, paving the way for more nuanced, multimodal audio processing systems, and thus more general and versatile audio intelligence.
items.zh: 从音频转录到音频描述。当前音频基础模型的预训练范式通常利用音频-文本预训练来弥合文本与音频之间的差距，其中文本通过ASR转录从音频（语音）获得。然而，文本转录侧重于口语单词的内容（说了什么），忽略了音频中的重要信息，如副语言信息（例如，情感、风格、音色、语调）、声学场景和非语言声音。因此，引入描述性文本（即音频描述）以更丰富的上下文描绘音频至关重要。结合音频的转录性和描述性文本，使模型不仅能更好地理解和生成口语，还能理解复杂的声学环境，为更细致入微的多模态音频处理系统铺平道路，从而实现更通用和更全面的音频智能。

items.en: Better Audio Representations. Current audio leverages semantic tokens or acoustic tokens as its representations. Semantic tokens are typically obtained by ASR-based auxiliary loss, which focuses on transcription-oriented information and fails to capture rich acoustic details crucial for understanding and generation. Acoustic tokens are typically learned by audio reconstruction loss, which focuses on description-oriented acoustic details and fails to capture abstractive semantic information that is crucial to bridge to text intelligence. A valuable research direction is to develop representations that integrate both transcription-oriented semantic information and description-oriented acoustic features, encompassing nuances like speaker identity, emotion, and environmental sounds while maintaining high-level abstractive information, which is paramount for more sophisticated audio understanding and generation.
items.zh: 更好的音频表示。当前音频使用语义标记或声学标记作为其表示。语义标记通常通过基于ASR的辅助损失获得，侧重于面向转录的信息，无法捕获对理解和生成至关重要的丰富声学细节。声学标记通常通过音频重建损失学习，侧重于面向描述的声学细节，无法捕获对连接文本智能至关重要的抽象语义信息。一个有价值的研究方向是开发集成面向转录的语义信息和面向描述的声学特征的表示，涵盖说话人身份、情感和环境声音等细微差别，同时保持高级抽象信息，这对于更复杂的音频理解和生成至关重要。

items.en: Throw Away ASR and TTS in Audio Modeling. Current audio foundation models rely heavily on ASR and TTS to generate training data in both the pre-training and fine-tuning stages. The quality of training data is constrained by the text recognition accuracy of ASR and expressiveness/diversity/quality of synthesized speech in TTS. In this way, the audio models behave like a sophisticated distillation of existing ASR and TTS systems. As a result, they can hardly achieve performance far beyond the ceiling of ASR/TTS and cannot achieve truly autonomous audio intelligence. An important future direction is to train audio models without relying on ASR/TTS-based pseudo audio data but relying on native audio data, which can result in much higher performance upperbound.
items.zh: 在音频建模中抛弃ASR和TTS。当前音频基础模型在预训练和微调阶段严重依赖ASR和TTS来生成训练数据。训练数据的质量受限于ASR的文本识别准确率以及TTS合成语音的表现力/多样性/质量。这样，音频模型的行为就像现有ASR和TTS系统的复杂蒸馏。因此，它们很难实现远超ASR/TTS上限的性能，也无法实现真正自主的音频智能。一个重要的未来方向是训练音频模型时不依赖基于ASR/TTS的伪音频数据，而是依赖原生音频数据，这可以带来更高的性能上限。
